title: GIS & Spatial Analysis
description: The GIS module enables researchers to visualize patient populations and clinical outcomes in geographic context. Spatial analysis tools and contextual layers support exploration of social determinants of health, environmental exposures, and geographic variation in care access and outcomes.
tips: Add contextual layers such as census demographics or environmental indices to enrich spatial analysis of clinical outcomes.
Use spatial clustering analysis to identify geographic regions with elevated disease burden or unmet care needs.
Ensure patient location data is appropriately de-identified or aggregated before performing and sharing geographic analyses.
Combine GIS findings with cohort-level data to investigate whether geography modifies treatment outcomes in your study population.
Export spatial analysis results in standard GIS formats for integration with external mapping and reporting tools.
